title: terminalia microcarpa subsp. microcarpa
intro: Terminalia microcarpa subsp. microcarpa, also known as the small-leaved terminalia or Indian blackberry, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Terminalia microcarpa subsp. microcarpa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional Indian cooking and as a garnish for various dishes.
